metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC3, e.g.: add a temporary command stub to `src/rebar/_commands`, run `python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`, assert `git diff --exit-code docs/cli-reference.md` exits non-zero, then revert — and document this as the acceptance test.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Adding/removing a CLI command or flag changes the generated output (verified)') has no proving command. 'Verified' is a hedge that does not specify who verifies it, by what mechanism, or what command produces evidence — an executor cannot confirm this criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Adding/removing a CLI command or flag changes the generated output (verified).'
findings.suggested_fix: AC3 proving command: `echo 'stub' >> src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py && python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py && git diff --exit-code docs/cli-reference.md; echo 'EXPECTED non-zero exit (diff detected)'; git checkout src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py docs/cli-reference.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the stated source of truth: replace 'CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)' with the actual sources — static help text in `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt`, the dispatch frozensets in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`, and per-command argparse parsers — and specify which of these `gen_cli_reference.py` will consume.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`' for deriving command names, usage strings, and flags, but the actual source of truth is `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt` (static package-data text files) plus dispatch frozensets in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`. No structured CLI registry exists in `_commands`.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the plan to say: 'derive the command inventory from the dispatch frozensets in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` and the static help text in `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt`; for commands with native argparse parsers (review, llm, workflow, etc.), invoke each with `--help` and capture stdout.' Remove the reference to a CLI registry in `_commands`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the generator's actual data sources: specify that gen_cli_reference.py must harvest from src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (for the canonical command list and usage strings), src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py (for all intercept arms and frozenset dispatch groups), AND src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py (_REGISTRY/_ARGV_REGISTRY for flags/arity), not solely from src/rebar/_commands.
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands' as the data source for the generator, but the actual authoritative command enumeration lives across three locations: (1) src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (known_subcommands() — the canononical list used by the CLI itself), (2) src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py (intercept arms for reconcile, review, review-code, scan-spec, verify-completion, review-plan, sign-review, enrich, explain, verify-commit-ticket, workflow, llm, jira-onboard, prompt, criteria, config, plus frozenset dispatch groups), and (3) src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py (_REGISTRY + _ARGV_REGISTRY, leaf-write only). A generator targeting only src/rebar/_commands would miss the majority of commands.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / --help (single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands' with: 'derive the command list from src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (the authoritative enumeration) and usage strings from the same package-data files; harvest arity/flag metadata from src/rebar/_commands.__init__._REGISTRY and _ARGV_REGISTRY for the leaf-write subset.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Restate the generator's source of truth as `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt` (the authoritative command enumeration that `known_subcommands()` already uses) plus `src/rebar/_cli/help/<cmd>.txt` for usage strings, covering all ~50 commands — not `src/rebar/_commands` alone, which is a partial leaf-write subset.
findings.finding: The plan names 'src/rebar/_commands' as the single source of truth for the command list, but this covers only ~13 of ~50 CLI commands. The actual authoritative source is src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (consumed by known_subcommands()); an additional ~15+ commands live in src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py intercept arms (reconcile, review, review-plan, workflow, llm, jira-onboard, config, explain, etc.) and read-path frozensets — none of these are in src/rebar/_commands. A generator built against _commands alone would produce an incomplete reference, and AC3 ('Adding/removing a CLI command or flag changes the generated output') would silently fail for any command in the _cli intercept layer.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / --help (single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands)'
findings.suggested_fix: Point gen_cli_reference.py at the package data in src/rebar/_cli/help/: enumerate known_subcommands() for the command list, read each <cmd>.txt for the usage string, and read overview.txt for the top-level structure. This gives complete, already-maintained coverage with zero subprocess invocation needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all dispatch paths the generator must cover (not just _REGISTRY and _ARGV_REGISTRY in _commands, but also the frozenset-routed reads, gates, lifecycle, bridge, IO, and direct-dispatch arms in _cli/__init__.py) and update the design to specify a complete enumeration strategy.
findings.finding: The plan asserts a single source of truth in 'src/rebar/_commands' but the actual CLI command surface is split across at least five distinct structures: _REGISTRY, _ARGV_REGISTRY (both in _commands/__init__.py), seven+ frozensets in _cli/__init__.py routing reads/gates/lifecycle/bridge/IO commands, and per-command argparse parsers. The generator design needed to cover the full surface is materially more complex than the env-registry prior art (which had one clean scan target), and the plan does not acknowledge this fragmentation.
findings.location: What section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / --help (single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the generator derives the full command list from `_help.known_subcommands()` / `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt` (the complete enumerable source) rather than `src/rebar/_commands`, which covers only the ~13 leaf-write commands.
findings.finding: The plan names `src/rebar/_commands` as the 'single source of truth' for the command list, but that module only registers ~13 leaf-write commands (`_REGISTRY` + `_ARGV_REGISTRY`). The actual full CLI surface (~50 subcommands) already lives as machine-readable package-data in `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt`, enumerable in-process via `_help.known_subcommands()` and `_help.subcommand_help(sub)`. Specifying `_commands` as the source would cause the generator to rebuild partial command-discovery from scratch while missing the majority of the CLI, when the full, authoritative enumeration already exists and is already used by tests.
findings.location: Plan body: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)'
findings.suggested_fix: In `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`, use `from rebar._cli._help import known_subcommands, subcommand_help, overview` to enumerate all subcommands and their usage strings. This reuses the existing machine-readable package-data already pinned by golden tests, rather than partially rebuilding command discovery from `_commands`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the generator's canonical source: either (a) enumerate all three locations (`_commands` registries + `_cli/__init__` intercept arms + `_cli/help/*.txt` package data) or (b) designate `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt` + `known_subcommands()` as the single source (already authoritative and complete) and derive usage strings from those files rather than re-invoking `--help`.
findings.finding: The plan's named source of truth — 'CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)' — is materially incomplete. The actual CLI surface spans at least three distinct locations: (1) `_REGISTRY` + `_ARGV_REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/_commands/__init__.py` (covers ~13 leaf-write commands); (2) hard-coded `if argv[0] == <sub>` intercept arms in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (covers `review`, `review-code`, `scan-spec`, `workflow`, `llm`, `reconcile`, `jira-onboard`, `prompt`, `criteria`, `config`, `verify-commit-ticket`, `sign-review`, `explain`, `enrich`, and others — none present in either registry); and (3) static `.txt` package-data files in `src/rebar/_cli/help/` (51 files — the authoritative known-subcommand catalog via `known_subcommands()`). A generator targeting only `src/rebar/_commands` will silently omit the majority of user-visible subcommands from the reference doc.
findings.location: What / Files section: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / `--help` (single source of truth in `src/rebar/_commands`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `rebar._cli._help.known_subcommands()` (which reads `src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt`) as the command catalog, and read each `<sub>.txt` for the usage string. This is already the complete, maintained, golden-pinned source of truth — the generator becomes a straightforward iteration over the package-data directory, exactly mirroring the env-registry scanner's proven pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `gerrit-verify.yaml` to the Files section and add or extend an AC to cover the Gerrit pipeline drift gate, or explicitly state it is out of scope.
findings.finding: The plan body names `gerrit-verify.yaml` as a CI file to update ('Wire a drift gate into CI mirroring the env-registry gate' referencing `gerrit-verify.yaml`), but the Files section omits it. This creates a contradiction between the body's described work and the declared deliverables list, leaving an implementer uncertain whether `gerrit-verify.yaml` is in scope.
findings.location: Plan body ('Wire a drift gate into CI mirroring the env-registry gate') vs. Files section (`.github/workflows/test.yml` only)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `gerrit-verify.yaml` to the Files list and extend AC2 to read: 'A CI drift gate (in both `test.yml` and `gerrit-verify.yaml`) fails the build if the committed reference is out of date.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` to the Files section as an edit target, since the plan body explicitly names it and the established env-registry drift-gate pattern requires the gate step in both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml.
findings.finding: gerrit-verify.yaml is referenced in the plan body ('Wire a drift gate into CI mirroring the env-registry gate', where the env-registry gate pattern explicitly includes gerrit-verify.yaml) but is absent from the Files section. The env-registry pattern the plan templates from updates both test.yml AND gerrit-verify.yaml — the Files list omits gerrit-verify.yaml as an edit target.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` and `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` to the Files list, and spec that the gate step must be wired into both workflows AND that `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` must be extended with the new gate's command signature.
findings.finding: The plan body mentions 'gerrit-verify.yaml' as a target for the drift gate, but the Files section omits it. This is a hidden ordering/completeness assumption: tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py has test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci(), which auto-detects any scripts/*.py added to test.yml and immediately fails unless the same script also appears in gerrit-verify.yaml. Additionally, _SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES in that same test must be extended with the new gate's signature. Without both updates, the implementation PR will fail test_ci_workflow_parity on its own CI run.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the generator's extraction mechanism: choose either (a) programmatic import/introspection of `src/rebar/_commands` objects or (b) subprocess `--help` calls, and record the decision in the plan.
findings.finding: The generator's data-extraction mechanism is ambiguous: the plan says 'derive… from the CLI registry / `--help`' without deciding between (a) programmatic import/introspection of `src/rebar/_commands` or (b) subprocess invocation of `--help`. These have different fragility, dependency, and portability profiles, and an executor would have to choose.
findings.location: What / body bullet: 'derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / `--help`'
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer programmatic introspection: 'The generator imports command objects from `src/rebar/_commands` directly (no subprocess) to extract names, usage strings, and flag metadata.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which source strategy the generator uses (structured registry introspection vs. --help text parsing) and justify the choice in the plan or a brief design note.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether the generator derives its data from the CLI registry (structured) or from --help output (text parsing), leaving an ambiguous and potentially brittle coupling point.
findings.location: ## What — bullet 1 ('derive the command list, usage strings, and flags from the CLI registry / --help')
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer structured registry introspection (importing/reading src/rebar/_commands programmatically) over --help text parsing; state this explicitly in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and explicitly list any existing hand-maintained CLI catalog files; add a step to delete or redirect them to `docs/cli-reference.md` so no stale references survive post-merge.
findings.finding: The plan announces replacement of 'hand-maintained CLI command catalogs' but names no specific existing files to delete or redirect, leaving them as potential stale references alongside the new generated doc.
findings.location: ## What — first paragraph ('Replace hand-maintained CLI command catalogs')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Files section: list each existing hand-maintained CLI doc (e.g. `docs/commands.md`, `COMMANDS.md`, or equivalent) with disposition 'deleted' or 'updated to redirect → docs/cli-reference.md'. Add an AC: 'No hand-maintained CLI catalog files remain after merge.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit error-handling requirements to the generator spec: validate that the discovered command list is non-empty, check subprocess exit codes, and exit non-zero (failing the CI step) on any introspection error.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no error handling for generator failure modes: un-importable CLI registry, commands with no `--help` output, or subprocess errors when introspecting commands. A silent failure (empty output or partial list) would produce a truncated reference that passes the 'generated' header check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the UPDATE path for `docs/cli-reference.md`: specify the exact command developers run to regenerate it (e.g., `make gen-cli-reference` or `python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`), and note whether a pre-commit hook or similar automation is in scope.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how or when developers regenerate `docs/cli-reference.md` before pushing. The update-ownership gap means developers may only discover staleness at CI time, with no guidance on the regeneration command, potentially causing repeated CI failures during iterative development.
findings.location: ## What — 'Wire a drift gate into CI'; Files list

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how docs/cli-reference.md relates to the existing src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt golden-pinned package data files: either derive from them as the single source (avoiding dual committed surfaces) or explicitly justify why a second artifact is preferable and how both will be kept in sync.
findings.finding: The plan's 'single source of truth in src/rebar/_commands' claim does not account for an existing committed CLI reference artifact: src/rebar/_cli/help/ contains 55 per-subcommand .txt files (including overview.txt) that already serve as byte-exact, golden-pinned CLI usage strings. The plan neither acknowledges this prior artifact nor explains how docs/cli-reference.md relates to or supersedes it — risking two competing committed CLI references.
findings.suggested_fix: The cleanest approach is to make gen_cli_reference.py read from src/rebar/_cli/help/*.txt (which are the actual `rebar --help` contract, not the internal _REGISTRY strings) and aggregate them into docs/cli-reference.md — this gives a genuine single source of truth and avoids fragmentation across _REGISTRY, _ARGV_REGISTRY, argparse parsers, and frozensets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default output structure for `docs/cli-reference.md` (e.g., H2 per command with synopsis, flags table, and one-line description) so the executor need not infer it.
findings.finding: The required content/schema of `docs/cli-reference.md` is unspecified beyond 'generated — do not edit' header. An executor must guess what sections (e.g., per-command synopsis, flags table, examples) the file must contain to satisfy AC1.
findings.location: AC1 / What bullet: 'emit `docs/cli-reference.md` with a "generated — do not edit" header'
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'Each command gets an H2 section with: one-line description, usage line, and a flags table (flag | type | default | description). Mirror the structure of `docs/env-registry.md` if one exists.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for AC1 (e.g., `python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py && git diff --exit-code docs/cli-reference.md` passes on a freshly generated file) and for AC2 (e.g., corrupt `docs/cli-reference.md` with a one-line edit, run the CI check locally with `act` or the equivalent, and confirm non-zero exit).
findings.finding: AC1 and AC2 lack explicit proving commands. AC1 ('docs/cli-reference.md is generated and marked generated') and AC2 ('a CI drift gate fails the build if the committed reference is out of date') are stated as outcomes but provide no command an executor can run to produce evidence of satisfaction.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: AC1 and AC2 lines
findings.suggested_fix: AC1: `python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py && git diff --exit-code docs/cli-reference.md` (exits 0 iff committed file matches generation). AC2: `echo 'STALE' >> docs/cli-reference.md && python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py --check` (non-zero exit proves gate fires); restore file after.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `gen_cli_reference.py` writes to a temp file and atomically renames it to `docs/cli-reference.md` (or equivalent safe-write pattern) to prevent partial-write corruption.
findings.finding: The generator script performs a file write (`docs/cli-reference.md`) with no specification of atomic/safe write behavior. A crash mid-write leaves a partially-written, committed file that the drift gate would treat as 'current,' silently corrupting the reference.
findings.location: scripts/gen_cli_reference.py (new) — file I/O section; AC: 'docs/cli-reference.md is generated'
findings.suggested_fix: In the script spec, add: write output to `docs/cli-reference.md.tmp`, then `os.replace('docs/cli-reference.md.tmp', 'docs/cli-reference.md')` — `os.replace` is atomic on POSIX and safe on Windows.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
